description_long: This stereo sound system features a classic wooden cabinet with a turntable on top, a central stack of audio components including a cassette deck and radio tuner, and two large, matching wooden speakers. The front of the cabinet has open shelving for vinyl records, adding both storage and a retro aesthetic. The speakers have visible woofers and tweeters, with black circular grilles and control panels on the upper sections. The wood finish is a rich, medium brown, giving the system a vintage, high-fidelity appearance. The control panel includes several knobs and buttons, likely for volume, tuning, and input selection. The overall design is robust and substantial, suitable for placement in a living room, garage, or urban recreational space.
description: A vintage-style wooden stereo sound system with a turntable, audio components, and two large matching speakers, featuring visible controls and record storage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the turntable, audio stack, open record storage, and the speaker drivers and controls.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the cabinet and the plain wooden sides of the speakers.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid wooden backs of the cabinet and speakers, with no visible controls or speaker drivers.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the wooden sides and the turntable's profile.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: The speaker grilles are circular and black, with a fine mesh or perforated pattern, and no visible decorative elements.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel is located on the front of the central audio stack, featuring several round knobs and rectangular buttons arranged in horizontal rows, with possible labeling above or below each control.
